questions.question: The Kaushalya Dam, which is important for flood control in the Ghaggar river basin, is situated in which Indian state?
questions.explanation: The Kaushalya Dam is located near Pinjore in Haryana on the Kaushalya river, a tributary of Ghaggar. It plays a vital role in flood management and water storage in the Ghaggar basin; Haryana is the only state with such a dam on this river.

questions.question: Painted Stork, that was recently seen in news, is mainly found in which region?
questions.explanation: The National Zoological Park has increased surveillance and biosecurity measures. This action follows the discovery of a painted stork carcass near a pond. Another painted stork was found sick in the water bird aviary. The painted stork is a large wading bird belonging to the stork family. It is easily recognized by its heavy yellow beak with a down-curved tip. These birds are found across the plains of tropical Asia. Their range extends from the Indian Subcontinent to Southeast Asia, south of the Himalayas. They prefer freshwater wetlands as their main habitat. They are also found in irrigation canals and flooded rice fields during the monsoon.

questions.question: Which Indian Naval Ship (INS) participated in Exercise Bright Star 2025, a multilateral drill hosted by Egypt from September 1 to 10?
questions.explanation: Indian naval stealth frigate INS Trikand arrived at Alexandria, Egypt on Monday for operational deployment in the Mediterranean Sea. INS Trikand participated in Exercise Bright Star 2025, a major multilateral drill hosted by Egypt from September 1 to 10. Exercise Bright Star is conducted under the aegis of U.S. Central Command to enhance interoperability, strengthen regional security, and address hybrid threat scenarios. Participating nations include India, Egypt, United States, Saudi Arabia, Qatar, Greece, Cyprus, and Italy. The exercise aims to deepen bilateral and multilateral ties among participating countries.

questions.question: Yuktdhara Portal is a Geographical Information System (GIS)-powered platform designed for which scheme?

options.label: D
options.text: Mahatma Gandhi National Rural Employment Guarantee Scheme

answer.label: D
answer.text: Mahatma Gandhi National Rural Employment Guarantee Scheme

questions.explanation: From 1st April 2026, Uttar Pradesh will integrate with the Yuktdhara Portal to monitor Mahatma Gandhi National Rural Employment Guarantee Scheme (MNREGS) works in real time. Yuktdhara Portal is a Geographical Information System (GIS)-powered geospatial planning system for village-level Mahatma Gandhi National Rural Employment Guarantee Scheme (MNREGS) planning across India. It records and maps the action plan, location, cost, and progress of every work. Integration ensures transparency and accountability, reducing payment anomalies and irregularities.

questions.question: Who has been honoured with Prof. V.K. Gokak Award 2025?
questions.explanation: Anand V. Patil has been honoured with Prof. V.K. Gokak Award. The award ceremony is held in Bengaluru on September 7 2025. The award is named after Prof. Vinayaka Krishna Gokak, a prominent Indian literary figure. It recognises individuals with significant contributions to Indian literature, especially in the Kannada language. The award highlights the importance of promoting regional literature and nurturing young readers. Anand V. Patil’s recognition reflects his lasting impact on children’s literary culture in India.

questions.question: Who won the women’s singles title at the US Open tennis Grand Slam 2025?
questions.explanation: World No. 1 Aryna Sabalenka won the US Open Tennis Women’s Singles title, beating Amanda Anisimova 6-3, 7-6. Sabalenka successfully defended her crown and claimed her fourth Grand Slam title. Spanish-Argentine duo Marcel Granollers and Horacio Zeballos won the Men’s Doubles title. They defeated British pair Joe Salisbury and Neal Skupski 3-6, 7-6, 7-5.

questions.question: What is the name of fitness campaign launched to mark Prime Minister Narendra Modi’s 75th birthday?
questions.explanation: Bharatiya Janata Yuva Morcha (BJYM) launched the ‘Namo Yuva Run’ to mark PM Narendra Modi’s 75th birthday on September 17. The nationwide run will be held on September 21 across 75 cities in India. More than 1 million youths are expected to participate in the campaign. The event promotes fitness and a drug-free India as its twin messages.

questions.question: The Giant African Snail (Lissachatina fulica), one of the world’s worst invasive species, has infested which Indian city?
questions.explanation: The Giant African Snail (Lissachatina fulica), one of the world’s worst invasive species, has infested Chennai city and its outskirts. Experts warn the snail spreads rapidly during floods, posing risks to city residents. It is not only an agricultural pest but also a carrier of dangerous parasites. The snail transmits parasitic nematodes Angiostrongylus cantonensis and A. costaricensis. These cause eosinophilic meningoencephalitis (brain infection) and abdominal angiostrongyliasis in humans. Infections spread through ingestion of contaminated snails or their residues. Researchers highlighted that Chennai faces a major public health threat due to this invasive species.

questions.question: India won the bronze medal in the CAFA (Central Asian Football Association) Nations Cup 2025 by defeating which country?
questions.explanation: India defeated Oman to secure third place and win the bronze medal in the CAFA (Central Asian Football Association) Nations Cup 2025. The match was held at Hisor Central Stadium in Tajikistan. The game ended 1-1 in regulation time after Udanta Singh scored the equaliser for India. In the penalty shoot-out, Oman missed their first two attempts. Indian goalkeeper Gurpreet Singh Sandhu saved the final penalty, giving India a 3-2 victory. This was the first-ever win for India against Oman in an international football match. The victory marked a historic and memorable finish to India’s CAFA 2025 campaign. Uzbekistan won the gold medal and Iran won the silver.

questions.question: Sankarshan Thakur, who recently passed away, was associated with which field?
questions.explanation: Sankarshan Thakur, Editor of The Telegraph, passed away on September 8, 2025, in a Gurgaon hospital at the age of 63. Thakur was known for incisive political analysis, fearless commentary, and sharp ground reporting. He authored famous books like Making of Laloo Yadav, The Unmaking of Bihar, Single Man: The Life and Times of Nitish Kumar, and The Brothers Bihari. He began his career with Sunday magazine of Ananda Bazar Patrika group. He worked with The Indian Express and Tehelka, later becoming National Affairs Editor and then Editor of The Telegraph. He won the Prem Bhatia Award (2001) for excellence in political journalism. His ground reporting in Manipur (2001) after the ceasefire extension with NSCN-IM was widely acclaimed.
